channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Breaking: ABC's Justin Stevens resigns as director of news
item.description: Justin Stevens, the ABC's director of news, has announced his resignation after 19 years with the organisation.

media:group.media:description: Justin Stevens has resigned from the ABC after 19 years.

item.title: 'About bloody time': BOM selects new NT radar site 11 years after closure
item.description: BOM says it has decided to install a new weather radar in Tennant Creek, in the same place as the previous radar.
item.dc:creator: Charles Geary and Stewart Brash

media:group.media:description: The original Tennant Creek radar, pictured before it was removed.

item.title: Since placing his first bet at age 15, Dylan says he's lost $900k
item.description: From footy tips to Taylor Swift bets, teenagers are being lured into gambling. Almost 34 per cent of Australian teens gamble and everyone from teachers, experts, to the young people themselves are sounding the alarm.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Safioleas

media:group.media:description: Almost 34 per cent of all teenagers in Australia gamble.

item.title: 'Inadequate' jail sentence for sex worker killer increased after appeal
item.description: Xiaozheng Lin will spend more time in prison for killing two sex workers in 2022, following an appeal by the prosecutor.
item.dc:creator: Kristian Silva

media:group.media:description: Xiaozheng Lin's sentence for killing two women has been increased.

item.title: Police 'reservations' about expanding powers of Jewish security groups
item.description: NSW Police Deputy Commissioner David Hudson has given evidence at the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion.
item.dc:creator: Phoebe Pin

media:group.media:description: Deputy Commissioner Hudson told the royal commission he held "considerable reservations" about expanding the powers of Jewish security groups.

item.title: Concussion saw Macy lose her 'spark', but a new trial helped her recover
item.description: Macy became a shell of her usual self after suffering a concussion on a school trip, but experienced a huge improvement after a trial designed to help children.
item.dc:creator: Kate O'Halloran

media:group.media:description: Macy Watson (left) pictured with her mum Jo. Macy suffered a concussion on a school trip.

item.title: Booming cockroach numbers welcome news on island 'paradise of creatures'
item.description: Researchers say the removal of invasive rodents on Lord Howe Island has sparked a rebound of invertebrate life, including native cockroaches and other insects, vital to the broader ecosystem.
item.dc:creator: Emma Siossian

media:group.media:description: Native bush cockroaches on Lord Howe Island have bounced back strongly after the rodent eradication on the island.

item.title: NASA has revealed its plan to live on the Moon. Here's how it would work
item.description: The US space agency has outlined plans for a sprawling lunar outpost involving rovers, hopping drones and resource-extraction technology as part of a renewed race to the Moon.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Courty

media:group.media:description: An artist's rendering showing astronauts, habitats, rovers, power systems and cargo operations at the proposed Moon base.

item.title: Domestic violence operation results in 70 arrests in SA
item.description: SA Police say they targeted 351 offenders as part of Operation Storm Days of Action, attending 443 addresses across the state in April.
item.dc:creator: Eugene Boisvert

media:group.media:description: The operation began in July 2024 targeting domestic violence.

item.title: More than a million worshippers brave searing heat for annual Hajj pilgrimage
item.description: Thousands of Muslim worshippers converge on the rocky hills and plains of Saudi Arabia's Mount Arafat as the annual Islamic pilgrimage, the Hajj, reached its peak.
item.dc:creator: Brianna Morris-Grant

media:group.media:description: More than a million Muslims are completing the annual Hajj pilgrimage near the holy city of Mecca.

item.title: Preferred bidder announced for troubled Liberty Bell Bay smelter
item.description: A consortium headed by Adroit Capital has been announced as the preferred bidder for the smelter in northern Tasmania, as federal and state government pledge another $5 million to support workers in the weeks ahead.
item.dc:creator: Clancy Balen

media:group.media:description: The smelter was placed in limited operations over a year ago.

item.title: Headline inflation eases to 4.2 per cent in April as fuel prices fall
item.description: Headline inflation decelerated in April, with consumer prices increasing at an annual pace of 4.2 per cent, down from 4.6 per cent in March.
item.dc:creator: Gareth Hutchens

media:group.media:description: Fuel prices fell 7 per cent in April, after the federal government halved the fuel excise to shield motorists from the global energy shock.

item.title: Young farmer who died two weeks after engagement set to leave lasting legacy
item.description: A scholarship will be created at a Victorian agricultural college in memory of a young man who died in a farming accident last month.
item.dc:creator: Angus Verley

media:group.media:description: Callum Smail and Brittany Scholz got engaged two weeks before the fatal accident.

item.title: Media leaks ahead of Ben Roberts-Smith arrest referred to corruption commission
item.description: News crews were at Sydney Airport as a plane carrying Ben Roberts-Smith landed, and captured images of his arrest from inside the terminal.
item.dc:creator: Tom Lowrey

media:group.media:description: Vision shared by authorities of Ben Roberts-Smith's arrest.

item.title: Two-time Ballon d'Or winner Putellas ends 14-year Barcelona career
item.description: Alexia Putellas ends her stint at Barcelona after winning 10 league titles and four Champions Leagues with the Spanish giants.

media:group.media:description: Alexia Putellas leaves Barcelona as a 10-time league champion and a two-time Ballon d'Or winner.

item.title: Birrell stuns fifth seed as Osaka and Sabalenka dazzle at French Open
item.description: Australia's Kimberly Birrell continues a day of upsets for Australia by advancing to the second round of the French Open for the first time in her career.

media:group.media:description: Kimberly Birrell has advanced to the second round of the French Open for the first time in her career.

item.title: Two men released without charge after Dezi Freeman-related arrest
item.description: A 48-year-old and a 45-year-old were taken into custody by detectives from Taskforce Summit, who continued to investigate Freeman's movements after the fatal shooting of officers Neal Thompson and Vadim de Waart-Hottart at a Porepunkah property in Victoria's north-east in August last year.

media:group.media:description: Dezi Freeman spent seven months on the run.

item.title: Outback school finally set to be rebuilt after mould forced demolition
item.description: More than two years since a health hazard led to Broken Hill's Willyama High School being knocked down, work on the "world-class" facility planned to replace is expected to begin.
item.dc:creator: Bill Ormonde

media:group.media:description: Tom Kennedy, Rebecca Milsteed, Local Government Minister Ron Hoenig, Barwon MP Roy Butler and Peter Macbeth turn the sod.

item.title: Koalas at risk of death once seven-day temps rise beyond 27C
item.description: The risks of death or hospitalisation of koalas increases as soon as seven-day average maximum temperature is above 27 degrees Celsius, a new study shows.
item.dc:creator: Peter de Kruijff

media:group.media:description: A koala tries to shelter from hot conditions in north-west NSW.

item.title: 'Not far away': Rescuers confident of reaching group trapped in Laos cave
item.description: Seven people remain trapped after heavy rain triggered a landslide but rescuers say they are close to reaching the group.

media:group.media:description: Rescuers have been working for days to free the trapped group.

item.title: The extraordinary legacy of a trailblazing female driving instructor
item.description: One of South Australia's first female licensed driving instructors has been remembered for her tireless advocacy and devotion to her community.
item.dc:creator: Jackson Byrne

media:group.media:description: Margaret Greenslade speaking at Renmark High School in 1972, where she officiated as a representative member of the school council in inducting the school prefects.

item.title: Town sites closed for testing after kids find liquid mercury vials at tip
item.description: A south-west Queensland town's school, service station, hospital waiting room and several homes are closed for testing after children found liquid mercury vials at the tip and took them to school.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Walsh

media:group.media:description: Several locations in Cunnamulla are being tested for liquid mercury contamination.

item.title: Employment services to get largest overhaul in 30 years
item.description: Jobseeker services will shift away from a one-size-fits-all approach to a new tiered model, to be unveiled by the government later today.
item.dc:creator: Holly Tregenza

media:group.media:description: The government's proposed new system will include three tiers of support for different types of jobseekers.

item.title: NT paramedics consider body-worn cameras amid ongoing violent assaults
item.description: The Northern Territory's ambulance service is considering introducing police-style body-worn cameras after the arrest of alleged murderer Jefferson Lewis led to rioting in Alice Springs last month.
item.dc:creator: Tilda Colling

media:group.media:description: St John Ambulance NT is considering putting further security measures in place to ensure the safety of their paramedics.

item.title: BP removes chair over 'serious concerns' about conduct
item.description: The BBC reported Albert Manifold's departure followed concerns about "bullying" and "overbearing" behaviour, which he later described as a "false narrative".
item.dc:creator: Tessa Flemming

media:group.media:description: Albert Manifold has been removed from his role eight months into his tenure.
